title: Add .dlp/deliverables/**/*.md negation rule to .gitignore (resolve SPEC.md conflict at root cause)
description: Append a single-line .gitignore negation rule (`!.dlp/deliverables/**/*.md`) below the blanket `*.md` exclusion so that per-deliverable SPEC.md files at `.dlp/deliverables/{deliverable-id}/SPEC.md` (canonical location per deliverable schema convention) commit without `git add -f`. Closes FU-EB-DLP-SPEC-MD-GITIGNORE-CONFLICT-1. Resolves at root cause rather than carrying force-add as a standing pattern, which would match the forbidden-paint anti-pattern (RULE-CI-DOCUMENTED-OVERRIDE-NOT-COMPLIANCE-1) wrapped around SPEC.md authoring across all future arc deliverables.

outputs.description: Append .gitignore negation rule for .dlp/deliverables/**/*.md immediately below the blanket *.md exclusion line. The negation rule MUST appear after the blanket so gitignore's later-rule-wins semantics apply correctly. Pattern: `!.dlp/deliverables/**/*.md`. May include adjacent annotation block (blank + comment lines) matching the file's existing annotated-negation style at lines 272–286. No other .gitignore rules touched; behavior for all other *.md paths preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Single semantic addition to .gitignore — the negation pattern `!.dlp/deliverables/**/*.md`, optionally with an adjacent annotation block (blank line + comment lines) matching the file's existing annotated-negation style at lines 272–286 (CLAUDE.md, README.md, infrastructure/docs/*.md negations all carry comment annotations explaining purpose)
Line appears AFTER the existing blanket `*.md` exclusion (verify with `grep -n '\*\.md' .gitignore` showing blanket first, negation second)
No other .gitignore lines modified, deleted, or reordered (verify with `git diff .gitignore` showing only the negation rule + optional adjacent annotation block as new lines)
Test the negation works: `touch .dlp/deliverables/test/SPEC.md && git check-ignore -v .dlp/deliverables/test/SPEC.md` returns exit code 1 (not ignored); cleanup the test file before staging
Test the blanket still works for other .md paths: `touch foo.md && git check-ignore -v foo.md` returns exit code 0 (still ignored); cleanup test file before staging
Test the negation is path-specific: `touch .dlp/scratch.md && git check-ignore -v .dlp/scratch.md` returns exit code 0 (still ignored — only .dlp/deliverables/**/*.md is allowed)

outputs.description: Author this deliverable's SPEC.md at .dlp/deliverables/d-2026-06-08-eb-dlp-spec-md-gitignore-negation/SPEC.md following the canonical convention. The file commits without `git add -f` because OUT-1's negation rule applies first in the same commit. Demonstrates the pattern works end-to-end and serves as the canary test for all future deliverable SPEC.md authoring.
acceptanceCriteria: SPEC.md includes: mission (resolve gitignore-vs-convention conflict at root cause), scope (single-line negation rule + FU closure), forbidden-paint anti-pattern reasoning, prerequisite chain (depends on PR 1.2 main + S7→S8 hygiene PR landing first so FU exists to close), n8n integrity note (Hard Constraint #1 trivially preserved — no DB anything)
SPEC.md commits via plain `git add` (no `-f` flag), confirming OUT-1 negation works
SPEC.md ends with mantra: Enterprise Excellence. Schema-First. No Shortcuts.

outputs.description: Close FU-EB-DLP-SPEC-MD-GITIGNORE-CONFLICT-1 in .roadmap/follow-up-register.json. Status open → closed. resolution.closedDate set to wall-clock, resolution.closedDuring set to this deliverable id, resolution.summary cites the root-cause resolution. resolution.commitSha and resolution.prUrl populated POST-merge via a separate backfill step (memory: same PENDING_POST_MERGE placeholder pattern as PR 1.2 closures). Indexes refreshed from followUps[]. meta.totalCount and meta.byStatus/byPriority synced. version patch-bumped. Top-level lastUpdated set.
acceptanceCriteria: FU-EB-DLP-SPEC-MD-GITIGNORE-CONFLICT-1 status field flips from 'open' to 'closed' in this commit

resolution block populated with required fields: closedDate (date format YYYY-MM-DD), closedDuring (string referencing this deliverable id), summary (cites root-cause resolution + this deliverable id)
resolution.commitSha = 'PENDING_POST_MERGE' placeholder (filled by sibling backfill PR or next-cycle hygiene PR)

SPEC.md committed via plain git add (no -f flag) — confirms OUT-1 negation works end-to-end

transitions.trigger: L4 named-step approval: 'I want enterprise best to proceed. do not propose patches or work arounds'

notes:
Sibling micro-PR to PR 1.2 (d-2026-06-08-eb-voxel-decision-aggregation-fix). Pattern instance of 'Repo Config Micro-PR' — root-cause resolution of a repo-level config conflict surfaced during main PR execution, separate from the S7→S8 Hygiene Micro-PR which is scope-locked to FU register state only.

File-and-close-on-tight-loop: FU-EB-DLP-SPEC-MD-GITIGNORE-CONFLICT-1 is filed by the S7→S8 hygiene PR (sibling) immediately after PR 1.2 main merges, and closed by THIS PR within the same 24h cycle. Net audit trail: FU has a real open→closed transition with cited resolution, not a forever-open tech-debt entry.

Rationale for root-cause resolution over force-add-and-log: force-add + standing FU is the precise shape of the forbidden-paint anti-pattern (RULE-CI-DOCUMENTED-OVERRIDE-NOT-COMPLIANCE-1) that the deliverable schema's forbidden-audit-paint-names constraint exists to reject. Carrying force-add as ongoing practice on every deliverable SPEC.md (T1, T2, T3, T4, T5, T6 of the URO arc and all subsequent arcs) compounds the anti-pattern across every child deliverable. Resolving at root costs ~one line + this small deliverable cycle; resolving at root pays back permanently.

Sequencing (strict order):
1. PR 1.2 main (d-2026-06-08-eb-voxel-decision-aggregation-fix) squash-merges
2. S7→S8 hygiene PR runs — files FU-EB-DLP-SPEC-MD-GITIGNORE-CONFLICT-1 among others
3. This PR's EB-CLI execution dispatch authored chat-side + sandbox-validated, then routed to EB-CLI
4. EB-CLI executes — applies OUT-1 .gitignore patch, then OUT-2 SPEC.md author, then OUT-3 FU closure
5. Squash-merge; SR-chat closes with TRUTH-DELIVERED

Hard Constraint #1 (n8n integrity): trivially preserved. This PR touches .gitignore, SPEC.md, and follow-up-register.json — no source code, no migrations, no schema mutations, no infrastructure config. n8n PostgreSQL pipeline at 159.223.203.231 cannot be affected by these changes.

Cross-repo doctrine F0B85LNSTC4: this spec gets posted to Slack channel C0B7EPFCA85 with file SHA256 + bytes + validation status; SR-chat picks up from channel and lands SR anchor at schemas/_instances/deliverables/d-2026-06-08-eb-dlp-spec-md-gitignore-negation.json on luh-tech/schema-registry. EB-CLI execution dispatch follows AFTER SR anchor lands per memory #24.

Enterprise Excellence. Schema-First. No Shortcuts.

--- AMENDMENT v1.0.1 (2026-06-08) ---

OUT-1 acceptance criterion #1 + #3 + description amended for substrate honesty. v1.0.0 was authored without inspecting actual `.gitignore` content; the spec language 'Exactly one line added' was too strict and inconsistent with the file's existing annotated-negation convention at lines 272–286 (CLAUDE.md, README.md, infrastructure/docs/*.md negations carry comment annotations explaining purpose). Substrate-confirm at execution P1.1 revealed the convention. v1.0.1 generalizes 'exactly one line' to 'single semantic addition with optional adjacent annotation block matching file convention'. The technical work (`!.dlp/deliverables/**/*.md` negation rule) is unchanged; only the acceptance criteria text precision is amended. Memory #20 substrate-honesty amendment pattern — established precedent at d-2026-06-05-eb-tier1-t1-foundation v1.0.1→v1.0.4 amendment chain. CLI's P1.2 execution (3-line annotated form: blank + 2 comment lines + rule) is consistent with v1.0.1 acceptance and SHOULD NOT be redone.
